title: Everything we know about Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot
excerpt: Tesla is preparing to build Optimus in Fremont. Here's what we know about the humanoid robot's price, capabilities, and biggest hurdles.
content_html:
Elon Musk built Tesla into the world's most valuable carmaker. Now he's betting the company's future on a robot that walks on two legs.

Musk predicts its robot, Optimus, could become "the biggest product ever," with legions of the humanoid robots working in factories, doing household chores, and eventually building more robots. He even has a name for them in the plural: "Optimi."

But Tesla has yet to prove the robot can navigate the physical world or perform useful work autonomously, much less that it can be manufactured by the millions.

On Tesla's earnings call this week, Musk offered few details about Optimus and tempered expectations for how quickly production would increase. He said Tesla would begin producing its third-generation robot "soon" in Fremont in the San Francisco Bay Area.

Tesla is competing in an increasingly crowded humanoid market. Agility Robotics, which plans to go public, has deployed its Digit robot across nine customer facilities, while Figure AI has announced deployments in logistics and distribution centers this year. Sunday Robotics, 1X, and Weave Robotics are preparing to ship robots into homes this fall.

Guy Hoffman, an associate professor of mechanical and aerospace engineering at Cornell University who leads its human-robot collaboration lab, called humanoids a "fantasy product."

Autonomous cars took about 20 years to reach the market after the technology was first shown to work, he told Business Insider. Building humanoids is even harder, and a fully autonomous machine has yet to be developed.

"Humanoid robots are a very risky bet," Hoffman said. "I don't see the product having a viable future in the near term."

Musk has never shied away from moonshot projects. But even he has acknowledged that Optimus is harder to develop than Tesla's Model X, Cybertruck, or gigafactories. If Tesla pulls it off, Musk believes Optimus could usher in "sustainable abundance," a future in which AI and robotics make human labor largely optional.

Tesla did not repond to a request for comment from Business Insider

Here's everything we know about Optimus so far:

From human to humanoid

Musk introduced the "Tesla Bot" in 2021 with a person dancing in a robot costume.

Three years later, Optimus robots danced, served drinks, and spoke with guests at Tesla's "We, Robot" event. The robots walked on their own, but more complex movements, such as pouring drinks, handing out desserts, and interacting with attendees, were assisted by remote human operators.

Tesla hasn't yet revealed what the next version of Optimus will look like. All we know is that it stands nearly six feet tall and has a humanlike design, a form that one Silicon Valley investor dismissed to Business Insider as a "parlor trick". Musk has defended humanoids, arguing that robots must resemble people to perform the full range of human tasks.

Chinese companies, including Unitree and UBTech, accounted for about 90% of humanoid shipments last year, according to technology research firm Omdia. Musk has argued that Optimus will be much more sophisticated than its Chinese rivals, but has shared few details so far.

"Optimus is designed to have a lot of intelligence and to have the same electromechanical dexterity, if not higher, than a human," Musk said on a podcast earlier this year. "Unitree does not have that."

Where Optimus stands now

Even though Tesla has not publicly unveiled the next-generation Optimus, it released photos this week of the Fremont production line, where manufacturing is expected to begin soon. Musk previously said that Tesla was keeping the robot under wraps to prevent competitors from copying its design.

Tesla stopped producing the Model S and Model X in Fremont earlier this year to make room for an Optimus line designed to eventually produce a million robots a year. A second line under construction in Austin is planned to have an annual capacity of 10 million robots.

The first bots will join Tesla's "Optimus Academy," where they will practice tasks and generate data used to improve the AI models that serve as their "brains." Musk has said that 10,000 to 30,000 robots will refine their real-world skills at the academy.

On the company's earnings call this week, AI chief Ashok Elluswamy said that the training could push the robots' capabilities to a "superhuman level."

Musk cautioned investors this week that production will ramp slowly because there is no established supply chain for many Optimus components.

Tesla has three big problems to solve

To make Optimus a reality, Musk says Tesla must solve three problems: intelligence, hands, and mass production.

First, the robot must learn to understand and move through the physical world. Musk believes Tesla's self-driving work gives it a head start because Optimus can use Tesla-designed AI chips and technology developed to help its cars interpret camera footage.

Tesla has adapted its driving simulator, a virtual environment used to train and test its self-driving technology, to train millions of virtual robots. But simulations cannot capture every real-world scenario, so Tesla also needs data from physical tasks. Tesla employees have recorded themselves performing factory jobs, for instance.

Hoffman, the Cornell professor, said Musk is underestimating how much harder humanoid robotics is than self-driving.

"It's like playing checkers versus doing nuclear physics," he said. Humanoids must balance on two legs without falling, he added, and today's AI models are still far from operating reliably in the physical world.

The second challenge is what Musk calls the "hands problem": replicating the human hand with motors, sensors, and software. A robot hand must combine strength, precision, and flexibility in a remarkably small space, making it exceptionally difficult to engineer.

On Tesla's earnings call this week, Musk said Optimus is being designed to have "human and then superhuman dexterity." Achieving that has required Tesla to develop custom motors, gears, and sensors.

The final challenge is scaling up manufacturing. Musk has warned that early production will be "agonizingly slow." But once Tesla starts producing a million robots a year — the timeline for which is still unclear — he estimates each Optimus could cost about $20,000 to $25,000 to produce.

How Optimus fits into Musk's AI empire

Musk has increasingly talked about a "convergence" across his business empire, which is becoming more intertwined around AI.

Ahead of and during Tesla's earnings call this week, shareholders and analysts pressed Musk about a rumored merger with SpaceX. Musk wouldn't comment on the speculation, but highlighted the company's synergies, including the Robotaxi and AI businesses.

Musk has previously explained how Optimus could work with SpaceX's technology, which acquired xAI in February. Each robot would have enough computing power to perform some tasks independently, while xAI's Grok could coordinate larger groups, he said.

"Let's say you wanted to build a factory," Musk said earlier this year. "Grok could organize the Optimus robots, assign them tasks to build the factory floor to produce whatever you want."

She writes about factory automation, humanoid robots, and the race to collect the real-world data needed to bring AI into the physical world. She previously worked at The San Francisco Standard, where she reported on tech culture and autonomous vehicles. She has a bachelor’s degree in history and politics from Pomona College and a master’s in history from the University of Cambridge. Rya lives in San Francisco. sentences.zh: 特斯拉

blocks.html_en: The first bots will join Tesla's "Optimus Academy," where they will practice tasks and generate data used to improve the AI models that serve as their "brains." Musk has said that 10,000 to 30,000 robots will refine their real-world skills at the academy.

sentences.en: The first bots will join Tesla's "Optimus Academy," where they will practice tasks and generate data used to improve the AI models that serve as their "brains."
sentences.zh: 第一批机器人将加入特斯拉的“擎天柱学院”，在那里他们将练习任务并生成用于改进充当“大脑”的人工智能模型的数据。

sentences.en: Musk has said that 10,000 to 30,000 robots will refine their real-world skills at the academy.
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示，10,000 至 30,000 台机器人将在学院中提高其现实世界技能。

blocks.html_en: On the company's earnings call this week, AI chief Ashok Elluswamy said that the training could push the robots' capabilities to a "superhuman level."

sentences.en: On the company's earnings call this week, AI chief Ashok Elluswamy said that the training could push the robots' capabilities to a "superhuman level."
sentences.zh: 在本周的公司财报电话会议上，人工智能负责人 Ashok Elluswamy 表示，训练可以将机器人的能力提升到“超人水平”。

blocks.html_en: Musk cautioned investors this week that production will ramp slowly because there is no established supply chain for many Optimus components.

sentences.en: Musk cautioned investors this week that production will ramp slowly because there is no established supply chain for many Optimus components.
sentences.zh: 马斯克本周警告投资者，由于许多 Optimus 组件没有既定的供应链，产量将缓慢增加。

blocks.html_en: Tesla has three big problems to solve

sentences.en: Tesla has three big problems to solve
sentences.zh: 特斯拉需要解决三大问题

blocks.html_en: To make Optimus a reality, Musk says Tesla must solve three problems: intelligence, hands, and mass production.

sentences.en: To make Optimus a reality, Musk says Tesla must solve three problems: intelligence, hands, and mass production.
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示，为了让擎天柱成为现实，特斯拉必须解决三个问题：智能、手工和量产。

blocks.html_en: First, the robot must learn to understand and move through the physical world. Musk believes Tesla's self-driving work gives it a head start because Optimus can use Tesla-designed AI chips and technology developed to help its cars interpret camera footage.

sentences.en: First, the robot must learn to understand and move through the physical world.
sentences.zh: 首先，机器人必须学会理解物理世界并在其中移动。

sentences.en: Musk believes Tesla's self-driving work gives it a head start because Optimus can use Tesla-designed AI chips and technology developed to help its cars interpret camera footage.
sentences.zh: 马斯克认为，特斯拉的自动驾驶工作使其处于领先地位，因为 Optimus 可以使用特斯拉设计的人工智能芯片和技术来帮助其汽车解读摄像机镜头。

blocks.html_en: Tesla has adapted its driving simulator, a virtual environment used to train and test its self-driving technology, to train millions of virtual robots. But simulations cannot capture every real-world scenario, so Tesla also needs data from physical tasks. Tesla employees have recorded themselves performing factory jobs, for instance.

sentences.en: Tesla has adapted its driving simulator, a virtual environment used to train and test its self-driving technology, to train millions of virtual robots.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉对其驾驶模拟器（用于训练和测试其自动驾驶技术的虚拟环境）进行了改造，以训练数百万个虚拟机器人。

sentences.en: But simulations cannot capture every real-world scenario, so Tesla also needs data from physical tasks.
sentences.zh: 但模拟无法捕捉所有现实场景，因此特斯拉还需要来自物理任务的数据。

sentences.en: Tesla employees have recorded themselves performing factory jobs, for instance.
sentences.zh: 例如，特斯拉员工记录了自己在工厂工作的情况。

blocks.html_en: Hoffman, the Cornell professor, said Musk is underestimating how much harder humanoid robotics is than self-driving.

sentences.en: Hoffman, the Cornell professor, said Musk is underestimating how much harder humanoid robotics is than self-driving.
sentences.zh: 康奈尔大学教授霍夫曼表示，马斯克低估了人形机器人比自动驾驶困难得多。

blocks.html_en: "It's like playing checkers versus doing nuclear physics," he said. Humanoids must balance on two legs without falling, he added, and today's AI models are still far from operating reliably in the physical world.

sentences.en: "It's like playing checkers versus doing nuclear physics," he said.
sentences.zh: “这就像玩跳棋和做核物理一样，”他说。

sentences.en: Humanoids must balance on two legs without falling, he added, and today's AI models are still far from operating reliably in the physical world.
sentences.zh: 他补充说，类人机器人必须用两条腿保持平衡而不会摔倒，而当今的人工智能模型距离在现实世界中可靠运行还很远。

blocks.html_en: The second challenge is what Musk calls the "hands problem": replicating the human hand with motors, sensors, and software. A robot hand must combine strength, precision, and flexibility in a remarkably small space, making it exceptionally difficult to engineer.

sentences.en: The second challenge is what Musk calls the "hands problem": replicating the human hand with motors, sensors, and software.
sentences.zh: 第二个挑战是马斯克所说的“手问题”：用电机、传感器和软件复制人手。

sentences.en: A robot hand must combine strength, precision, and flexibility in a remarkably small space, making it exceptionally difficult to engineer.
sentences.zh: 机器人手必须在非常小的空间内将力量、精度和灵活性结合起来，这使得它的设计变得异常困难。

sentences.en: Tesla has not unveiled the latest version of Optimus to prevent competitors from copying its design.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉尚未公布最新版本的 Optimus，以防止竞争对手抄袭其设计。

sentences.en: Costfoto/NurPhoto via Getty Images
sentences.zh: Costfoto/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

blocks.html_en: On Tesla's earnings call this week, Musk said Optimus is being designed to have "human and then superhuman dexterity." Achieving that has required Tesla to develop custom motors, gears, and sensors.

sentences.en: On Tesla's earnings call this week, Musk said Optimus is being designed to have "human and then superhuman dexterity."
sentences.zh: 在特斯拉本周的财报电话会议上，马斯克表示，擎天柱的设计目的是拥有“人类乃至超人的灵活性”。

sentences.en: Achieving that has required Tesla to develop custom motors, gears, and sensors.
sentences.zh: 要实现这一目标，特斯拉需要开发定制电机、齿轮和传感器。

blocks.html_en: The final challenge is scaling up manufacturing. Musk has warned that early production will be "agonizingly slow." But once Tesla starts producing a million robots a year — the timeline for which is still unclear — he estimates each Optimus could cost about $20,000 to $25,000 to produce.

sentences.en: The final challenge is scaling up manufacturing.
sentences.zh: 最后的挑战是扩大制造规模。

sentences.en: Musk has warned that early production will be "agonizingly slow."
sentences.zh: 马斯克警告称，早期生产将“极其缓慢”。

sentences.en: But once Tesla starts producing a million robots a year — the timeline for which is still unclear — he estimates each Optimus could cost about $20,000 to $25,000 to produce.
sentences.zh: 但一旦特斯拉开始每年生产 100 万台机器人（具体时间尚不清楚），他估计每个 Optimus 的生产成本可能约为 20,000 至 25,000 美元。

blocks.html_en: How Optimus fits into Musk's AI empire

sentences.en: How Optimus fits into Musk's AI empire
sentences.zh: 擎天柱如何融入马斯克的人工智能帝国

blocks.html_en: Musk has increasingly talked about a "convergence" across his business empire, which is becoming more intertwined around AI.

sentences.en: Musk has increasingly talked about a "convergence" across his business empire, which is becoming more intertwined around AI.
sentences.zh: 马斯克越来越多地谈论他的商业帝国的“融合”，该帝国与人工智能的联系越来越紧密。

blocks.html_en: Ahead of and during Tesla's earnings call this week, shareholders and analysts pressed Musk about a rumored merger with SpaceX. Musk wouldn't comment on the speculation, but highlighted the company's synergies, including the Robotaxi and AI businesses.

sentences.en: Ahead of and during Tesla's earnings call this week, shareholders and analysts pressed Musk about a rumored merger with SpaceX.
sentences.zh: 在特斯拉本周的财报电话会议之前和期间，股东和分析师就与 SpaceX 合并的传闻向马斯克施压。

sentences.en: Musk wouldn't comment on the speculation, but highlighted the company's synergies, including the Robotaxi and AI businesses.
sentences.zh: 马斯克没有对这一猜测发表评论，但强调了该公司的协同效应，包括机器人出租车和人工智能业务。

blocks.html_en: Musk has previously explained how Optimus could work with SpaceX's technology, which acquired xAI in February. Each robot would have enough computing power to perform some tasks independently, while xAI's Grok could coordinate larger groups, he said.

sentences.en: Musk has previously explained how Optimus could work with SpaceX's technology, which acquired xAI in February.
sentences.zh: 马斯克此前曾解释过 Optimus 如何与 SpaceX 的技术合作，该公司于 2 月份收购了 xAI。

sentences.en: Each robot would have enough computing power to perform some tasks independently, while xAI's Grok could coordinate larger groups, he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，每个机器人都有足够的计算能力来独立执行一些任务，而 xAI 的 Grok 可以协调更大的群体。

blocks.html_en: "Let's say you wanted to build a factory," Musk said earlier this year. "Grok could organize the Optimus robots, assign them tasks to build the factory floor to produce whatever you want."

sentences.en: "Let's say you wanted to build a factory," Musk said earlier this year.
sentences.zh: “假设你想建一家工厂，”马斯克今年早些时候表示。

sentences.en: "Grok could organize the Optimus robots, assign them tasks to build the factory floor to produce whatever you want."
sentences.zh: “Grok 可以组织 Optimus 机器人，给它们分配任务来建造工厂车间，生产你想要的任何东西。”

sentences.en: Have a tip?
sentences.zh: 有小费吗？

blocks.html_en: Rya is a senior reporter at Business Insider covering physical AI and robotics. She writes about factory automation, humanoid robots, and the race to collect the real-world data needed to bring AI into the physical world. She previously worked at The San Francisco Standard, where she reported on tech culture and autonomous vehicles. She has a bachelor’s degree in history and politics from Pomona College and a master’s in history from the University of Cambridge. Rya lives in San Francisco. Contact her at rjetha@businessinsider.com or on Signal at rjetha.07. Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device. Here's our guide to sharing information securely.

sentences.en: Rya is a senior reporter at Business Insider covering physical AI and robotics.
sentences.zh: Rya 是《商业内幕》的资深记者，报道物理人工智能和机器人技术。

sentences.en: She writes about factory automation, humanoid robots, and the race to collect the real-world data needed to bring AI into the physical world.

blocks.html_en: • Robotics

sentences.en: Robotics
sentences.zh: 机器人技术

blocks.html_en: • Tesla
• Elon Musk

sentences.en: Elon Musk
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克

blocks.html_en: • More
• Electric Vehicles

sentences.en: Electric Vehicles
sentences.zh: 电动车

content_html_zh:
埃隆·马斯克将特斯拉打造成世界上最有价值的汽车制造商。 现在，他将公司的未来押在两条腿行走的机器人上。

马斯克预测，其机器人擎天柱可能会成为“有史以来最大的产品”，大批人形机器人在工厂工作、做家务，并最终制造出更多机器人。 他甚至给它们起了一个复数的名字：“Optimi”。

但特斯拉尚未证明该机器人可以在物理世界中导航或自主执行有用的工作，更不用说它可以批量生产了。

在特斯拉本周的财报电话会议上，马斯克几乎没有提供有关 Optimus 的细节，并缓和了对产量增长速度的预期。 他表示，特斯拉将“很快”在旧金山湾区的弗里蒙特开始生产第三代机器人。

特斯拉正在日益拥挤的人形机器人市场中展开竞争。 计划上市的 Agility Robotics 已在 9 个客户设施中部署了 Digit 机器人，而 Figure AI 也宣布今年在物流和配送中心进行部署。 Sunday Robotics、1X 和 Weave Robotics 正准备在今年秋天将机器人送入千家万户。

康奈尔大学机械与航空航天工程系副教授盖伊·霍夫曼（Guy Hoffman）领导着该校的人机协作实验室，他称类人机器人是一种“幻想产品”。

他告诉 Business Insider，自该技术首次被证明有效以来，自动驾驶汽车花了大约 20 年的时间才进入市场。 建造人形机器人更加困难，而且完全自主的机器尚未开发出来。

“人形机器人是一个非常冒险的赌注，”霍夫曼说。 “我认为该产品在短期内不会有可行的未来。”

马斯克从不回避登月项目。 但就连他也承认，Optimus 比特斯拉的 Model X、Cyber​​truck 或超级工厂更难开发。 马斯克相信，如果特斯拉成功，擎天柱可能会迎来“可持续的丰富”，在这个未来，人工智能和机器人技术将在很大程度上使人类劳动力成为可有可无的选择。

特斯拉没有回应《商业内幕》的置评请求

以下是迄今为止我们所了解的有关 Optimus 的所有信息：

从人类到类人生物

马斯克在 2021 年推出了“特斯拉机器人”，其中一个人穿着机器人服装跳舞。

三年后，擎天柱机器人在特斯拉的“我们，机器人”活动中跳舞、提供饮料并与客人交谈。 机器人可以自行行走，但更复杂的动作，例如倒饮料、分发甜点以及与与会者互动，则需要远程操作员的协助。

特斯拉尚未透露下一版本的 Optimus 会是什么样子。 我们所知道的是，它高近六英尺，具有人性化的设计，一位硅谷投资者向《商业内幕》认为这种形式是“客厅把戏”。 马斯克为类人机器人辩护，认为机器人必须类似于人类才能执行人类的全部任务。

根据技术研究公司 Omdia 的数据，包括 Unitree 和 UBTech 在内的中国公司去年约占人形机器人出货量的 90%。 马斯克认为，擎天柱将比其中国竞争对手更加先进，但迄今为止尚未透露太多细节。

马斯克在今年早些时候的播客上表示：“擎天柱被设计为拥有很高的智能，并且具有与人类相同甚至更高的机电灵活性。” “Unitree 没有这个。”

擎天柱现在所处的位置

尽管特斯拉尚未公开推出下一代 Optimus，但它本周发布了弗里蒙特生产线的照片，预计很快就会开始生产。 马斯克此前表示，特斯拉正在对机器人保密，以防止竞争对手抄袭其设计。

今年早些时候，特斯拉停止在弗里蒙特生产 Model S 和 Model X，为 Optimus 生产线腾出空间，该生产线最终旨在每年生产 100 万台机器人。 奥斯汀正在建设的第二条生产线计划年产能1000万台机器人。

第一批机器人将加入特斯拉的“擎天柱学院”，在那里他们将练习任务并生成用于改进充当“大脑”的人工智能模型的数据。 马斯克表示，10,000 至 30,000 台机器人将在学院中提高其现实世界技能。

在本周的公司财报电话会议上，人工智能负责人 Ashok Elluswamy 表示，训练可以将机器人的能力提升到“超人水平”。

马斯克本周警告投资者，由于许多 Optimus 组件没有既定的供应链，产量将缓慢增加。

特斯拉需要解决三大问题

马斯克表示，为了让擎天柱成为现实，特斯拉必须解决三个问题：智能、手工和量产。

首先，机器人必须学会理解物理世界并在其中移动。 马斯克认为，特斯拉的自动驾驶工作使其处于领先地位，因为 Optimus 可以使用特斯拉设计的人工智能芯片和技术来帮助其汽车解读摄像机镜头。

特斯拉对其驾驶模拟器（用于训练和测试其自动驾驶技术的虚拟环境）进行了改造，以训练数百万个虚拟机器人。 但模拟无法捕捉所有现实场景，因此特斯拉还需要来自物理任务的数据。 例如，特斯拉员工记录了自己在工厂工作的情况。

康奈尔大学教授霍夫曼表示，马斯克低估了人形机器人比自动驾驶困难得多。

“这就像玩跳棋和做核物理一样，”他说。 他补充说，类人机器人必须用两条腿保持平衡而不会摔倒，而当今的人工智能模型距离在现实世界中可靠运行还很远。

第二个挑战是马斯克所说的“手问题”：用电机、传感器和软件复制人手。 机器人手必须在非常小的空间内将力量、精度和灵活性结合起来，这使得它的设计变得异常困难。

在特斯拉本周的财报电话会议上，马斯克表示，擎天柱的设计目的是拥有“人类乃至超人的灵活性”。 要实现这一目标，特斯拉需要开发定制电机、齿轮和传感器。

最后的挑战是扩大制造规模。 马斯克警告称，早期生产将“极其缓慢”。 但一旦特斯拉开始每年生产 100 万台机器人（具体时间尚不清楚），他估计每个 Optimus 的生产成本可能约为 20,000 至 25,000 美元。

擎天柱如何融入马斯克的人工智能帝国

马斯克越来越多地谈论他的商业帝国的“融合”，该帝国与人工智能的联系越来越紧密。

在特斯拉本周的财报电话会议之前和期间，股东和分析师就与 SpaceX 合并的传闻向马斯克施压。 马斯克没有对这一猜测发表评论，但强调了该公司的协同效应，包括机器人出租车和人工智能业务。

马斯克此前曾解释过 Optimus 如何与 SpaceX 的技术合作，该公司于 2 月份收购了 xAI。 他说，每个机器人都有足够的计算能力来独立执行一些任务，而 xAI 的 Grok 可以协调更大的群体。

“假设你想建一家工厂，”马斯克今年早些时候表示。 “Grok 可以组织 Optimus 机器人，给它们分配任务来建造工厂车间，生产你想要的任何东西。”

Rya 是《商业内幕》的资深记者，报道物理人工智能和机器人技术。 她撰写的文章涉及工厂自动化、人形机器人，以及收集将人工智能带入物理世界所需的现实世界数据的竞赛。 她此前曾在《旧金山标准报》工作，负责报道科技文化和自动驾驶汽车。 她拥有波莫纳学院历史和政治学学士学位以及剑桥大学历史学硕士学位。 Rya 住在旧金山。 请通过 rjetha@businessinsider.com 或通过 Signal 联系她：rjetha.07。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备。 这是我们安全共享信息的指南。
